scenarios.title: Sleep: multi-turn continuity
scenarios.objective: Verify Talk V2 carries bedtime context across multiple turns without restarting.
turns: Bedtime is taking over an hour every night. What should I change first?
We tried earlier bedtime and it got worse. What should we do instead?
Can you summarize a 3-night experiment based on what I already told you?

scenarios.title: Anxiety: multi-turn continuity
scenarios.objective: Verify continuity and calm tone across repeated anxiety concerns.
turns: My child wakes up anxious about school most mornings.
The breathing script worked once but now she refuses. Next step?
How do I talk to school tomorrow using the same plan?

scenarios.title: School: follow-up conversations
scenarios.objective: Verify follow-up behavior references prior school details.
turns: School says he is masking all day and crashing at home.
I need one short email to teacher and one home adjustment.
Update: teacher agreed to the break card. What should I track this week?

scenarios.title: Meltdown: escalation then recovery
scenarios.objective: Verify support through escalation and debrief recovery.
turns: We had a major meltdown after homework and everyone is exhausted.
He started yelling and throwing things. What is the immediate reset plan?
Now that he is calm, how do I repair without making it worse next time?

scenarios.title: Parent exhaustion: emotional support
scenarios.objective: Verify tone warmth and practical support without generic reset replies.
turns: I am so tired and I feel like I am failing every day.
Please do not give me a long list. I can do one thing tonight.
Can you help me plan tomorrow morning with low energy?

scenarios.title: PDA demand avoidance: several follow-ups
scenarios.objective: Verify continuity over several follow-up turns on demand language and autonomy.
turns: Every request becomes a battle. I think demand avoidance is driving this.
When I say 'put shoes on' it escalates instantly. Rephrase options?
He rejected both options. What is a third path that keeps autonomy?
Can you give me a one-minute script I can use before school?

scenarios.title: Medication: information seeking
scenarios.objective: Verify balanced informational support and safe boundaries.
turns: We started ADHD medication this week and afternoons are rough.
What patterns should I log before our clinician follow-up?
How do I describe side effects clearly without overreacting?

scenarios.title: General parenting: normal conversation
scenarios.objective: Verify normal, practical, non-scripted conversational flow.
turns: We are trying to build a calmer evening routine for the whole family.
Can you help me choose between visual checklist or timer-first approach?
Great, now give me a short version my partner can follow too.
